name: healthcare_municipal_bond_covenant_kestrel_car_fair
premise: A hospital incident command bridge convened after a critical infrastructure or patient-flow failure with competing clinical priorities. Bed capacity, consent, and chart documentation are all under real-time scrutiny. The situation maps to internal runbooks and external messaging tied to: healthcare municipal bond covenant kestrel car fair. The moral tension is explicitly framed as care versus fairness: leadership cannot fully satisfy both without accepting tradeoffs. Insurance and risk are insisting on a paper trail before any irreversible action; operations says delay costs measurable harm. A wire cutoff and settlement window closes in under thirty minutes; reversals may be impossible until the next business day. Any exception must be time-boxed, named, and reversible where technically feasible. Anyone affected by an automated hold or model output must retain a human escalation path that is actually staffed.

agents.name: Mira Navarro
agents.identity: I am Mira Navarro, the accountable decision-maker on this Healthcare bridge; I own what gets logged, communicated, and executed in the next window.
agents.goal: I need to stop the bleeding without baking in a rule that later punishes fairness whenever someone invokes care.

agents.name: Omar Hughes
agents.identity: I am Omar Hughes, responsible for surfacing who gets protected last when systems fail; I am watching for silent harm and unfair distribution.
agents.goal: I want safeguards so fairness is not sacrificed by default just because care feels morally loud in the moment.

choices: I will choose a narrow, time-boxed authorization with explicit human override, subgroup monitoring, and a hard stop if harm indicators rise—accepting slower relief to protect fairness.

I will choose a broad immediate authorization to stabilize outcomes fast, with transparent metrics and rapid correction afterward—accepting higher risk to fairness to secure care before the window closes.
forced_choice.call_to_action: The clock on this bridge is running out. With partial evidence, Mira Navarro must decide under care versus fairness. What does Mira Navarro do?
